parsing_approach: Lexical extractor (these files open with a `<%@ ... %>` directive, which is not well-formed XML, so no real parser is possible with stdlib). Extracts every directive and its attributes via regex; classifies CodeBehind/CodeFile as file references and Inherits/Class/Service as type references so the semantic-graph builder can attempt to resolve them against actual C# constructs.
extracts: the file's entry directive kind (Application, ServiceHost, WebHandler, WebService, Page, Control, Master, ...) and its Language attribute, recorded on the file node
one Directive node per `<%@ ... %>` occurrence with all attributes captured as attr_* properties
FileReference node + references edge for CodeBehind/CodeFile attribute values (unresolved relative paths)
TypeReference node + references edge for Inherits/Class/Service attribute values (unresolved qualified type names)
inline `<script runat="server">` blocks by presence, span, and language attribute — contents are not parsed
known_limitations: Lexical only: attribute values that span multiple lines with nested `%>`-like sequences inside quoted strings could confuse the non-greedy directive match, though this does not occur in practice for these files.

FileReference and TypeReference targets are recorded as raw strings; resolving them to actual File/Class nodes (e.g. produced by csharp-structure) is the semantic-graph builder's job, not this parser's.

Inline server-script C# code is detected but not parsed — pair with csharp-structure conceptually if that code matters, though in practice ASP.NET MVC entry points (Global.asax, .svc) in this codebase use CodeBehind exclusively.
Secondary directives (Assembly, Import, Register, OutputCache) are captured generically like any other directive kind but their specific semantics are not interpreted.
